steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Apply the pyramid volume formula
steps.content:
The volume of a pyramid is $V = \frac{1}{3} \times \text{base area} \times \text{height}$. For a square base with side length $s$, the base area is $s^2$, so:
$$V = \frac{1}{3}s^2h$$
Substitute the given values $V = 300$ and $h = 9$:
$$300 = \frac{1}{3}s^2(9)$$

steps.title: Simplify and solve for the side length
steps.content:
Simplify the right side:
$$300 = \frac{9s^2}{3}$$
$$300 = 3s^2$$
Divide both sides by 3:
$$s^2 = 100$$
Take the positive square root:
$$s = 10$$
That matches Choice A — $10$.
